sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 6.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
sections.subtitleText: 6.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 64.2g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
sections.subtitleText: 64.2g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 14.1g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
sections.subtitleText: 14.1g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 49% daily value, iron at 25% daily value, zinc at 18% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 49% daily value, iron at 25% daily value, zinc at 18% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: unusually strong protein case for a grain. designed to expose whether grain proteins rescue too hard. good mineral support. better than many grains. strong healthy-grain reputation. very likely audience debate food.

displayItems.title: unusually strong protein case for a grain
displayItems.explanation: Designed to expose whether grain proteins rescue too hard.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first: unusually strong protein case for a grain. designed to expose whether grain proteins rescue too hard. good mineral support. better than many grains. strong healthy-grain reputation. very likely audience debate food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: GI is only decent. not elite on carb behaviour. prep quality matters. can be unpleasant if badly prepared. can be expensive vs staples. practical downside.

closing.summary: Quinoa does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
